definition: Rates volatility (MOVE index) has diverged from FX volatility by 1.8 standard deviations. Historical resolution pattern: 4/5 prior episodes resolved via FX vol repricing higher. Current episode duration (~18 months) exceeds median persistence of prior inversions (~12 months). However, against a 1973-2007 baseline, current G10 FX vol (7-9%) is ~35th-40th percentile, making the anomaly partially a reference period artifact.
evidence: 1.8 sigma divergence between MOVE and FX vol
4/5 historical episodes resolved via FX vol repricing
Current episode ~18 months vs median ~12 months persistence
2005-06 precedent of similar divergence preceded carry unwind
Against 1973-2007 baseline, current vol is 35th-40th percentile, not suppressed
4/4 historical episodes resolved via FX vol repricing higher
Against 1973-2007 baseline: G10 FX vol at 35th-40th percentile — unremarkable
Count discrepancy between agents (4/5 vs 4/4) flagged in pair_0
Structural arguments for permanently lower vol (algorithmic liquidity, CB communication, automated hedging) are plausible but untested
open_questions: Correct reference distribution for vol anomaly detection (the single most important unresolved methodological issue per challenger_02)
Whether structural changes in FX market microstructure permanently altered vol dynamics
Whether the 2005-06 precedent is the only valid prior or if the sample should be expanded
What is the correct reference period for FX vol — the single most important unresolved methodological question
Have FX markets permanently structurally changed post-2010?
Will the n=4 historical pattern hold given potential structural breaks?
How much of the divergence is explained by forward guidance vol suppression?
